The URL is valid for 300 seconds (5 minutes) and must be used before expiration.

get.summary: Get aggregated statistics for failure reports
get.description:
Returns aggregated statistics and dimensional breakdowns for DMARC failure reports.

Features:
- Aggregate by multiple dimensions (reporter, ptrGroup, countryCode, domain)
- Filter by domain names, date range, source IP, country code, reporter domain
- Control top N results per dimension
- Set minimum count threshold
- Sort by count or key
- Summary statistics including total reports, unique domains, unique reporters

Authentication required.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Organization ID to filter reports by

parameters.name: domains
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of domain names to filter reports by (minimum 1 required)

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Aggregate reports from this date (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Aggregate reports until this date (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: reportDate
parameters.description: Filter by specific report date (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: ptr
parameters.description: Filter by PTR record (must be "Unknown" or a valid domain)

parameters.name: ptrGroup
parameters.description: Filter by PTR group (must be "Unknown" or a valid domain)

parameters.name: dimensions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of dimensions to aggregate by (minimum 1 required, must be unique)

parameters.name: topN
parameters.description: Number of top results to return per dimension

parameters.name: minCount
parameters.description: Minimum count threshold for results (filters out entries below this count)

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: Field to sort aggregation results by

200.description: Returns aggregated statistics and dimensional breakdowns

post.description:
Generates a short-lived, one-time URL that authenticates a specific user into a specific organization, then redirects them to your chosen EasyDMARC URI.

## Using the Magic Link
1. Your backend calls POST /auth/magic-link with userId, organizationId, redirectUri.
2. API returns a single-use url and expiringAt.
3. Redirect the user (HTTP 302) or render a button that navigates to url.
4. The user is authenticated and then redirected to redirectUri within EasyDMARC.

Notes
- Treat the returned url as a credential: never log or expose it broadly.
- The link expires at expiringAt; do not cache beyond that time.
- A new link should be generated per session/initiation.


## Examples

**cURL**
```
curl -X POST "https://api2.easydmarc.com/auth/magic-link" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $EASYDMARC_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "userId": "c99cf9f3-f946-4707-9984-a19bf2d57841",
    "organizationId": "org_0987654321",
    "redirectUri": "https://account-console.easydmarc.com"
  }'
```

Security Best Practices
- Send the request server-to-server only; never expose your API token to the browser or client apps.
- Do not log full magic links; if needed, mask them.
- Prefer short validity windows for operational usage; launch the redirect immediately after generating the link.

requestBody.description: Magic link generation request with user, organization, and redirect information

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid input or missing parameters

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing Bearer token

post.description: Authenticates your client credentials and returns a bearer token required for accessing protected API endpoints.

200.description:
Authenticated. A successful request returns a bearer token and related metadata:

**Response Fields**
- access_token (string): The token used to authenticate API requests. Include this in the Authorization header as Bearer <access_token>.
- expires_in (number): Token validity duration in seconds (e.g. 300 = 5 minutes).
- refresh_expires_in (number): Always 0 – token refresh is not supported.
- token_type (string): Always Bearer.
- not-before-policy (number): Internal policy value (typically 0), safe to ignore.
- scope (string): Scope of access for the token (e.g. public-api).

get.description:
Query audit logs with comprehensive filtering options.

      **Filtering by user:** Use 'userIds' parameter with comma-separated user IDs or 'userEmails' parameter with comma-separated user emails.

      **Filtering by domain:** Use 'domainIds' parameter with comma-separated domain IDs.

      **Filtering by domain group:** Use 'domainGroupIds' parameter with comma-separated domain group IDs.

      **Filtering by action category:** Use 'actionCategories' parameter with one or more of:
      - DOMAINS: Domain-related actions
      - DOMAIN_GROUPS: Domain group actions
      - MANAGED_SERVICES: Managed DMARC, BIMI, MTA-STS, EasySPF actions
      - USER_MANAGEMENT: User invitation, revocation, permission changes
      - BILLING: Billing details, plan upgrades, cancellations
      - SECURITY_FEATURES: MFA setup/removal
      - ORGANIZATION_SECURITY: SSO setup, account termination
      - LOGIN_AND_LOGOUT: Login, logout, device actions
      - INTEGRATIONS: Integration connections, token generation
      - ALERTING: Alert creation, modification, deletion
      - RUF: Failure report actions
      - RUA: Aggregate report actions
      - PERSONAL_SETTINGS: Name changes
      - REPORTING: Scheduled report actions, whitelabel, SMTP setup
      - LABEL_MANAGEMENT: Label creation, modification, deletion

      **Filtering by specific actions:** Use 'actions' parameter with specific AuditLogAction values.

      **Date range:** Use 'startDate' and 'endDate' parameters (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Organization ID to query audit logs for

parameters.name: actionCategories
parameters.description: Filter by action categories (DOMAINS, DOMAIN_GROUPS, MANAGED_SERVICES, USER_MANAGEMENT, BILLING, SECURITY_FEATURES, ORGANIZATION_SECURITY, LOGIN_AND_LOGOUT, INTEGRATIONS, ALERTING, RUF, RUA, PERSONAL_SETTINGS, REPORTING, LABEL_MANAGEMENT)

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the date range filter

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the date range filter

403.description: Request is not authorized.

Returns parsed DMARC RUA (aggregate) reports with comprehensive
filtering and field selection capabilities.

Features:
- Filter by date range, domain names, report type, and custom filters
- Optional field selection (properties parameter)
- Flexible sorting with multiple order fields
- Pagination support (page/pageSize based)
- Advanced filtering with operators (eq, ne, in, nin, gt, lt, etc.)

Authentication required. Response is paginated.

get.description:
Retrieves a single parsed DMARC RUA (aggregate) report by its
unique UUID identifier.

Returns the complete report data including authentication results,
policy information, and source details. Authentication required.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID v4 of the DMARC report

post.description:
Retrieves distinct values for a specific DMARC report field
within the specified criteria.

Features:
- Get unique values for any DMARC report field
  (policy_domain, org_name, country_code, etc.)
- Filter by date range, domains, and report types
- Advanced filtering with operators
- Pagination support with configurable page size (max 100)
- Useful for populating dropdowns, filters, and data exploration

Authentication required. Results are paginated.

Returns aggregated DMARC authentication and disposition
statistics within specified criteria.

Features:
- Aggregate by SPF/DKIM authentication results (pass/fail counts)
- Aggregate by DMARC disposition (none/quarantine/reject counts)
- Filter by date range, domains, and report types
- Group results by any DMARC report field
- Advanced filtering with operators
- Flexible sorting with multiple order fields
- Pagination support
- Returns structured aggregation counts for analysis and reporting

Authentication required. Results are paginated.

Retrieves aggregated email volume data for domains within a
specified date range.

Features:
- Group results by any field except 'count'
  (policy_domain, org_name, source_ip, etc.)
- Filter by authentication results, disposition, country, and more
- Support for multiple report types and domains
- Flexible sorting with multiple order fields
- Pagination with configurable page size (max 100)
- Returns partial DmarcReportDto objects focused on volume aggregation

Authentication required. Results are paginated.

Returns volume data aggregated by time periods (day, week, month)
within a specified date range.

Features:
- Time-series volume data with configurable periods (day/week/month)
- Group results by any DMARC report field
  (policy_domain, country_code, etc.)
- Filter by authentication results, disposition, source, and more
- Support for multiple report types and domains
- Flexible sorting with multiple order fields
- Pagination support
- Each result includes volume count, date period, and grouping fields

Authentication required. Results are paginated.

200.description: Returns volume data by report period

post.summary: Get volume data by report period

post.description:
Returns DKIM and SPF authentication pass rates for domains
within a specified date range.

Features:
- Calculate pass rates for SPF and DKIM authentication
- Support for sender domains and parked domains
  (at least one must be provided)
- Filter by date range and custom filters
- Pagination support with configurable page size (max 100)
- Returns percentage-based pass rates for analysis and reporting
- Useful for domain authentication performance monitoring

Authentication required. Results are paginated.

Invites one or more users to join an organization by sending them invitation emails. The invited users will receive an email with a link to accept the invitation and set up their account.

**Features:**
- Bulk user invitation support
- Role assignment during invitation
- Custom redirect URL after invitation acceptance
- Automatic email notification to invited users

**Permissions Required:**
- Must have admin role in the organization
- Must have user management permissions

200.description: User invitation(s) sent successfully. Invitation emails have been dispatched to all specified addresses.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid input parameters, malformed email addresses, or missing required fields

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Authenticated but lacking permission to invite users to this organization

post.description:
Creates one or more new users in the organization with specified roles and permissions. Unlike the invite endpoint, this creates users directly without requiring them to accept an invitation.

**Key Differences from Invite:**
- Creates users immediately without invitation flow
- Users are active immediately
- No email invitation is sent

**Use Cases:**
- Bulk user import from external systems
- Automated user provisioning via integrations
- Pre-creating accounts for known team members

**Permissions Required:**
- Must have admin role in the organization
- Must have user creation permissions

201.description: User(s) created successfully. Returns a mapping of email addresses to their newly created user IDs.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid input parameters, malformed email addresses, or missing required fields

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Authenticated but lacking permission to create users in this organization

409.description: Conflict - One or more users already exist with the provided email addresses

get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific user by their user ID within an organization context. Returns comprehensive user profile data including personal information, verification status, and avatar details.

**Returned Information:**
- User identification (ID, username, email)
- Personal details (first name, last name)
- Account status (email verification)
- Profile customization (avatar, avatar color)

**Permissions Required:**
- Must have access to the specified organization
- Must have user read permissions

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the organization context. Used to verify the user's membership and access permissions.

200.description: User information retrieved successfully. Returns complete user profile data.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid user ID or organization ID format

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Authenticated but lacking permission to view this user's information

404.description: Not found - User does not exist or is not a member of the specified organization

post.description:
Revokes organization access for one or more specified users. This removes users from the organization and terminates their access to all organization resources.

**Important Notes:**
- This action is immediate and cannot be undone
- Users will lose access to all organization resources
- User accounts are not deleted, only their organization membership is removed
- Users can be re-invited or re-added to the organization later

**Permissions Required:**
- Must have admin role in the organization
- Cannot revoke access for yourself
- Cannot revoke access for the organization owner

200.description: Access revoked successfully. The specified users no longer have access to the organization.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid input parameters, malformed user IDs, or empty user list

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Authenticated but lacking permission to revoke access, or attempting to revoke own access

404.description: Not found - One or more specified users or the organization does not exist

patch.description:
Updates personal information for a specific user. This endpoint allows modification of user profile details such as first name and last name.

**Updatable Fields:**
- First name
- Last name

**Non-Updatable Fields:**
- Email address (requires separate verification flow)
- User ID
- Username
- Email verification status

**Permissions Required:**
- Must be the user themselves, or
- Must have admin role in the organization

200.description: User information updated successfully. Returns the updated user data.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid input parameters, empty update object, or validation errors

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Authenticated but lacking permission to update this user's information

404.description: Not found - User with the specified ID does not exist

organizationId.description: Organization identifier associated with the user.

expiringAt.description: Expiration time of the generated link.

client_id.description: Client ID you obtained in API Client management in Account Console

client_secret.description: Client Secret you obtained in API Client management in Account Console

access_token.description: The token used to authenticate API requests. Include this in the Authorization header as Bearer <access_token>.

expires_in.description: Token validity duration in seconds (e.g. 300 = 5 minutes).

refresh_expires_in.description: Always 0 - token refresh is not supported.

not-before-policy.description: Internal policy value (typically 0), safe to ignore.

scope.description: Scope of access for the token (e.g. public-api).

limit.description: Maximum number of items per page

total.description: Total number of items across all pages

hasPreviousPage.description: Whether there is a previous page available

hasNextPage.description: Whether there is a next page available

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the organization was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the organization was last updated

domain_count.description: Maximum number of domains that can be monitored under this plan

email_volume_count.description: Maximum email volume count allowed per month (in millions or total emails)

verification_count.description: Number of email verifications available per month

data_retention_days.description: Number of days the user can look back when viewing data. Does not affect data retention — only limits the visible date range (e.g., 30, 90, 365)

public_api.description: Whether public API access is enabled for programmatic integration

saml.description: Whether SAML-based Single Sign-On authentication is available

alerting.description: Whether email alerting and notification features are enabled

api_access.description: Whether API access is granted for this plan (legacy field, prefer public_api)

reputation_monitoring.description: Whether reputation monitoring and blacklist checking features are available

mailbox_count.description: Number of mailboxes that can be configured for inbox placement testing

inbox_placement_test_count.description: Number of inbox placement tests that can be run per month

easy_spf.description: Whether EasySPF feature for simplified SPF record management is enabled

domain_access.description: Whether domain-level access control and permissions are available

audit_log.description: Whether audit logging functionality for compliance and security tracking is enabled

audit_log_duration.description: Duration in days that audit logs are retained (e.g., 30, 90, 365)

email_investigation.description: Whether email investigation and forensics features are available

amazon_ses_integration.description: Whether Amazon SES integration for sending emails is enabled

touchpoint.description: Whether touchpoint tracking and engagement analytics are available

custom_dashboards.description: Whether custom dashboards feature is enabled

reputation_intelligence_integrations.description: Whether reputation intelligence integrations feature is enabled

email_preview_suite.description: Whether email preview suite feature is enabled

alert_management.description: Whether alert management feature is enabled

sso.description: Whether SSO (Single Sign-On) feature is enabled

esp_integrations.description: Whether ESP integrations (Amazon SES, SendGrid, Mailgun) are enabled

domainCount.description: Number of domains included in the plan

publicApi.description: Whether public API access is enabled

inboxPlacementTestCount.description: Number of inbox placement tests allowed

domainAccess.description: Whether domain access feature is enabled

auditLog.description: Whether audit log feature is enabled

auditLogDuration.description: Audit log retention duration in days

emailInvestigation.description: Whether email investigation feature is enabled

amazonSesIntegration.description: Whether Amazon SES integration is enabled

espIntegrations.description: Whether ESP integrations (Amazon SES, SendGrid, Mailgun) are enabled

id.description: The unique identifier of the price

name.description: The display name of the price

minimumBillableItems.description: Minimum number of billable items required for this price. Used for MSP/volume-based pricing.

billingCycles.description: Number of billing cycles for the price. If null, the subscription renews indefinitely.

billingPeriod.description: The billing period value combined with frequency. For example, 1 with frequency "month" means monthly billing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the price was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the price was last updated

id.description: The unique identifier of the plan

name.description: The display name of the plan

features.description: The feature set of the plan including domain limits, email volume, and feature flags

prices.description: The list of prices associated with the plan. Each price represents a different billing cycle (monthly, yearly) with its amount, currency, and billing period.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the plan was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the plan was last updated

id.description: Unique identifier of the subscription item

quantity.description: The quantity of the subscription item. Default is 1, mainly used for MSP prices.

features.description: The feature set of the subscription item. This is denormalized from the plan features but can be changed during subscription updates.

priceId.description: The unique identifier of the price this subscription item belongs to

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription item was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription item was last updated

id.description: The unique identifier of the subscription

cancelledAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription was cancelled. If null, the subscription is active.

items.description: The list of subscription items. Each subscription has at least one item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription was last updated

priceId.description: The unique identifier of the price to assign to the organization

organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization to assign the subscription to

priceId.description: The unique identifier of the new price for the subscription upgrade/downgrade

totalCount.description: Total number of records matching the query

key.description: The key of the validation message.

errors.description: The validation messages for the DNS record.

warnings.description: The warning messages for the DNS record.

info.description: The info messages for the DNS record.

DnsRecordStatus.description: The status of the BIMI TXT record.

domain.description: The domain name of the A record.

validation.description: The validation of the A record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the A record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

domain.description: The domain name of the AAAA record.

validation.description: The validation of the AAAA record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the AAAA record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

domain.description: The domain name of the NS record.

validation.description: The validation of the NS record.

queryStatus.description: The status of the NS record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the NS record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

priority.description: The priority of the MX record

addresses.description: The list of IP addresses associated with the exchange server

domain.description: The domain name of the MX record.

validation.description: The validation of the MX record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the MX record lookup.

domain.description: The domain name for which MX records were retrieved

records.description: List of MX records for the domain

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

ip.description: The domain name of the PTR record.

validation.description: The validation of the PTR record.

queryStatus.description: The status of the PTR record lookup.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the PTR record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

domain.description: The domain name of the TXT record.

validation.description: The validation of the TXT record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the TXT record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

domain.description: The domain name of the CNAME record.

validation.description: The validation of the CNAME record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the CNAME record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: Max age of the cache -1:[from cache] 0:[force lookup] ( in ms)

type.description: The type of the DMARC record term

tag.description: The tag associated with the DMARC record term

value.description: The value of the DMARC record term

p.description: The DMARC policy for the domain.

pct.description: The percentage of messages subjected to DMARC policy.

sp.description: The subdomain policy for the domain.

np.description: The non-compliant policy for the domain.

policyStatus.description: The policy status for the domain.

pct.description: The percentage of messages subjected to DMARC policy.

reportingStatus.description: Indicates whether DMARC reporting is enabled.

warnings.description: The list of errors found while parsing the DMARC record.

errors.description: The list of warnings found while parsing the DMARC record.

info.description: The list of info found while parsing the DMARC record.

domain.description: The domain name associated with the DMARC record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the DMARC record retrieval.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

recommendedRua.description: Recommended RUA address for record from platform.

domain.description: Domain name for the DKIM record

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the DKIM record lookup.

The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

success.description: Indicates whether the validation was successful.

recordTerms.description: Detailed information about SPF record terms

source.description: Detailed information about SPF record source

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

BimiRecordTermEnum.description: The type of the BIMI record term

tag.description: The tag associated with the BIMI record term

value.description: The value of the BIMI record term, which can be a Buffer, string, or specific term value type

size.description: The size of the SVG file.

title.description: The title of the SVG file.

base_profile.description: The base profile of the SVG file.

version.description: The version of the SVG file, optional.

url.description: The URL associated with the metadata.

metaData.description: The metadata object, which can be null.

validTo.description: The date when the certificate becomes invalid.

validFrom.description: The date when the certificate becomes valid.

issuerText.description: Text describing the issuer of the certificate.

domainNames.description: Domain names associated with the certificate.

serialNumber.description: The serial number of the certificate.

trademarkText.description: Text describing the trademark associated with the certificate.

organizationName.description: The organization name associated with the certificate.

url.description: The URL associated with the metadata.

metaData.description: The metadata object, which can be null.

warnings.description: The list of errors found while parsing the BIMI record.

errors.description: The list of errors found while parsing the BIMI record.

info.description: The list of errors found while parsing the BIMI record.

domain.description: The domain name of the BIMI record.

validation.description: The validation of the BIMI record.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the BIMI record lookup.

maxAgeMs.description: The maximum age of the cache in milliseconds [0]-fetch dns, [-1]-from cache

policyDomain.description: Domain for which the policy applies

count.description: Number of messages matching this report entry

countryCode.description: ISO country code of source IP

pct.description: Percentage tag value from DMARC policy

value.description: Value to filter by (type depends on operator)

domainNames.description: Array of fully qualified domain names

reportType.description: Type of DMARC reports to retrieve

filters.description: Advanced filtering options keyed by field name

properties.description: Optional array of specific fields to return in response

orderFields.description: Array of fields and directions for sorting results

domainNames.description: Array of fully qualified domain names

reportTypes.description: Array of report types to include

property.description: The DMARC report field to get distinct values for

filters.description: Advanced filtering options keyed by field name

spfPassQty.description: Count of emails with SPF pass

dkimPassQty.description: Count of emails with DKIM pass

spfFailQty.description: Count of emails with SPF fail

dkimFailQty.description: Count of emails with DKIM fail

dispositionNoneQty.description: Count of emails with DMARC disposition 'none'

dispositionRejectQty.description: Count of emails with DMARC disposition 'reject'

dispositionQuarantineQty.description: Count of emails with DMARC disposition 'quarantine'

domainNames.description: Array of fully qualified domain names

reportTypes.description: Array of report types to include

aggregations.description: Array of field-value pairs to aggregate by

filters.description: Advanced filtering options keyed by field name

groupByFields.description: Fields to group aggregation results by

orderFields.description: Array of fields and directions for sorting results

domainNames.description: Array of fully qualified domain names

reportTypes.description: Array of report types to include in volume calculation

filters.description: Advanced filtering options keyed by field name

groupByFields.description: Fields to group volume data by (excludes count field)

orderFields.description: Array of fields and directions for sorting results

policyDomain.description: Domain for which the policy applies

count.description: Number of messages matching this report entry

countryCode.description: ISO country code of source IP

pct.description: Percentage tag value from DMARC policy

qty.description: Volume quantity for the period (same as count)

date.description: Date representing the time period (YYYY-MM-DD format)

domainNames.description: Array of fully qualified domain names

reportTypes.description: Array of report types to include

filters.description: Advanced filtering options keyed by field name

groupByFields.description: Fields to group volume data by

orderFields.description: Array of fields and directions for sorting results

dkimPassRate.description: DKIM authentication pass rate as a percentage (0.0-100.0)

spfPassRate.description: SPF authentication pass rate as a percentage (0.0-100.0)

filters.description: Advanced filtering options keyed by field name

senderDomainNames.description: Array of sender domain names (optional if parkedDomainNames is provided)

parkedDomainNames.description: Array of parked domain names (optional if senderDomainNames is provided)

url.description: URL to the user's avatar image

firstName.description: Updated first name for the user

lastName.description: Updated last name for the user

userId.description: The unique identifier of the user whose information should be updated

info.description: Object containing the user information fields to update. Only provided fields will be updated.

username.description: Username for the user account, typically the email address or a unique identifier

firstName.description: First name of the user. May be "No name" if not set.

lastName.description: Last name of the user. May be "No name" if not set.

emailVerified.description: Indicates whether the user's email address has been verified

avatar.description: Avatar image information for the user. May contain URL or image data.

avatarColor.description: Color scheme for the user's avatar when no image is set. Used for generating colored initials.

organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization to which users are being invited

permissions.description: Permission configuration including user list and role assignments

redirectUrl.description: URL to redirect users to after they accept the invitation and complete onboarding. Must be a valid HTTPS URL.

organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization in which to create the users

permissions.description: Permission configuration including user details and role assignments

users.description: Map of user email addresses to their newly created user IDs

organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization from which to revoke user access

userIds.description: Array of user IDs whose access should be revoked. All users must be members of the specified organization.
